channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Beats just launched a genuinely useful accessory for your Apple tech – and it's not what you might expect
item.description: Beats has followed up last year's iPhone case with some more accessories –this time a range of colorful, braided data cables for iPhones, iPads, AirPods and more.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta antitrust trial tests MAGA-tech relationship
item.description: A major antitrust trial against Meta that kicked off on Monday — with CEO Mark Zuckerberg taking the stand — is a test of how much tech giants can get out of the MAGA embrace that swept across the industry in the wake of President Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: 6 tips for dealing with prima donna IT superstars
item.description: Most CIOs will tell you there are three or four people in their organizations who deliver enormous value to IT and are worth every dollar they get. They’re the superstars of databases, data management, security, applications, infrastructure, and systems.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk’s X faces EU probe over GDPR violations in AI training
item.description: Elon Musk’s X is facing a regulatory probe in Europe over its alleged use of public posts from EU users to train its Grok AI chatbot – an investigation that could set a precedent for how companies use publicly available data under the bloc’s privacy laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why digital transformation starts with an intelligent network infrastructure
item.description: Heightened end-user expectations and a greater emphasis on digitalization are rewiring the business landscape again.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI launches new GPT-4.1 models with improved coding
item.description: OpenAI on Monday launched its new AI model GPT-4.1, along with smaller versions GPT-4.1 mini and GPT-4.1 nano, touting major improvements in coding, instruction following, and long context comprehension.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok travel influencers are illegally hitching a ride on Mauritania’s Iron Ore Train
item.description: Mauritania isn’t typically a major tourist destination. But its only railway has recently become the subject of a viral TikTok travel trend: riding the “Iron Ore Train.” This 437-mile journey through the Sahara desert offers dramatic selfie backdrops—and no shortage of controversy.
item.dc:creator: Regina Borsellino, Fast Company

item.title: America’s loss is China’s gain: Beijing is recruiting fired U.S. scientists
item.description: China appears to be pursuing a calculated effort to recruit recently laid-off U.S. scientists—particularly those with expertise in artificial intelligence—to relocate or contribute remotely to research operations based in Shenzhen.
item.dc:creator: Tim Bajarin, Fast Company

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand at Meta trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took the stand Monday, as his social media company faces off against the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) in a case that could determine the fate of the tech titan’s empire.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Silver Lake Buys 51% Stake in Intel's Altera Unit for $8.75 Billion - Breaking News
item.description: Intel Corp. agrees to sell a 51% stake in its programmable chips unit Altera to Silver Lake Management, valuing the business at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: What to know about the all-female Blue Origin space launch
item.description: Blue Origin successfully launched an all-female, celebrity-packed crew on a brief trip into space, with the mission led by Blue Origin and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos' fiancé Lauren Sánchez.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 7 reasons low-code and no-code tools fail to deliver
item.description: The potential benefits of low-code and no-code development tools include faster application development, lower expenses, and more agility. The technology is not suited to every scenario, however, and in some cases, low- and no-code solutions could be a barrier to productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inside ‘Signalgate’: How The Atlantic navigated a national security scandal
item.description: When government officials accidentally included Jeffrey Goldberg, The Atlantic’s editor-in-chief, in a Signal group chat discussing U.S. military plans, all hell broke loose.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, WGN 9

item.title: Man who used AI lawyer explains why he did so after judge got angry
item.description: Dewald maintains he received prior approval for his presentation method, though not specifically for an AI-generated speaker.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, ABC 4
